data.body:
PASS-1 review (G6 — approach soundness). Findings:

- [ ] (G6) The plan's central landmine — the inert `# noqa: BLE0001` typo at `grounding/harness.py:281` — already appears FIXED in the current tree: the noqa reads `BLE001` (no extra 0), the catch is narrowed to `except Exception` (KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit propagate), and the reason string already cites the narrowing. Re-confirm the live state before implementing; if already remediated, drop the typo-fix from scope rather than re-apply.

- [ ] (G6) Fitness gap: AC "These packages removed from the allowlist" is unqualified, but `pyproject.toml:215` + the allowlist comment (lines 196-201) classify `_engine_support` T201 as LEGITIMATE machine-contract stdout (`reads_cli`/`validate`/`next_batch` emit `print(json.dumps(...))`). Removing `_engine_support` from T201 wholesale would break the data-CLI contract or force ~40 inline `# noqa: T201`. Plan should state explicitly: migrate only stderr DIAGNOSTIC prints to the logger; KEEP the stdout data-contract prints (scoped T201 ignore), drop only BLE001 for `_engine_support`.

- [ ] (G6) Missing positive rationale: the plan states WHAT (observability floor, migrate prints, shrink allowlist) but not WHY this strangler/per-package-sweep approach is chosen over alternatives. A one-line rationale (e.g. "mirrors the established epic ring-gun-jot shrinking-allowlist pattern; per-package sweep keeps blast-radius bounded and matches the 8d01/ceaa/fd72 precedent") would satisfy the positive-rationale criterion.
